summary: Backend testing complete for SWR span control feature. All 13 tests passed (100%). Verified that swr_span_mhz parameter correctly controls the frequency range of SWR curve: 5MHz span generates 61 points from 24.685-29.685 MHz, 10MHz span from 22.185-32.185 MHz, 20MHz span from 17.185-37.185 MHz. Default (no span) uses channel-based sweep of 0.6MHz (±30 channels). Y-axis max SWR correctly reaches 10 for wide spans.
critical_code_review_comments: models.py line 148: swr_span_mhz field correctly defined as Optional[float] with ge=0.1, le=20.0 validation
physics.py lines 1647-1656: Sweep range logic correctly handles swr_span_mhz - generates 61 points when provided, otherwise uses ±30 channels at channel_spacing intervals
physics.py lines 1651-1654: span calculation uses half_span = swr_span/2, num_points=61, sweep_step = swr_span/(num_points-1) for evenly-spaced points
physics.py lines 1710-1715: SWR curve derived from Smith Chart impedance data with values capped at 10.0
SwrMeter.tsx lines 21-23: Y-axis auto-scaling based on maxSwr (<=3→yMax=3, <=5→yMax=5, else yMax=10)
seed_data_creation: None - tests use inline payload data
context_for_next_testing_agent: SWR span feature verified working. Key test data: swr_span_mhz=5→61 points spanning 5MHz (24.685-29.685), swr_span_mhz=10→10MHz span (22.185-32.185), swr_span_mhz=20→20MHz span (17.185-37.185) with max SWR=10. Default (no span) uses channel-based 0.6MHz sweep with channel indices -30 to +30. Validation correctly rejects span<0.1 or span>20.

swr_span_mhz=5.0 returns 61 points spann.evidence: 61 points from 24.685 to 29.685 MHz (span 5.0 MHz, ±2.5MHz from center 27.185)

Default (no swr_span_mhz) uses channel-b.evidence: 61 points from 26.885 to 27.485 MHz (span 0.6 MHz), channel indices -30 to +30

swr_span_mhz=20.0 returns points with SW.evidence: 61 points from 17.185 to 37.185 MHz, min SWR 1.18, max SWR 10.0

SWR curve center matches matched_swr.evidence: Curve min SWR 1.16 close to matched_swr 1.256 (within tolerance)
